text.title: Embedding Model Selection for RAG Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the embedding-model-selection methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Embedding model + dimension count MUST be locked in a decision-record before any ingestion job runs.

rationale.source: OpenAI Embeddings docs 2026; Qdrant migration notes

rule.rationale: Switching model post-ingestion invalidates the entire vector index — full re-embedding is required and old vectors silently mismatch.

rule.statement: Store model_name and dimension_count in each chunk's metadata at ingestion time.
rule.rationale: Without metadata, migration planning is impossible — you cannot tell which chunks need re-embedding when the model changes.

rule.statement: If query_languages != document_languages OR ≥2 languages in either set, pick a multilingual model (embed-multilingual-v3.0 or bge-m3).
rule.rationale: Monolingual English models collapse cross-language semantic structure; cross-lingual recall drops > 40%.

rule.statement: Dimension truncation (1024 from 3072) is only safe on Matryoshka-trained models (text-embedding-3-*); other models lose monotonic quality on truncation.

rationale.source: OpenAI text-embedding-3 announcement; Matryoshka representation paper

rule.rationale: OpenAI Matryoshka representation preserves ~95% quality at 1/3 dims; truncating non-Matryoshka models corrupts the geometry.

rule.statement: If corpus < 500 docs OR keyword/BM25 retrieval meets SLA, skip this methodology and use lexical search.
rule.rationale: Embeddings introduce $/latency overhead with no recall lift on tiny static corpora.
